title: malus communis var. austera
intro: Malus communis var. austera, also known as the crab apple or wild apple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its fruit, leaves, and bark. The medicinal properties of Malus communis var. austera have been found to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and protecting against infections. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas or added to topical applications for wound care and skin issues.
